entries.reasoning_summary: Confirmed the working directory before any project initialization or scaffolding.
Checked worktree state early to avoid overwriting unrelated changes and to understand repo scope.

entries.stdout: Stripe Projects CLI v0.0.53 available with init, catalog, add, link, env, billing, and llm-context commands.
entries.reasoning_summary: Verified the requested CLI plugin is installed and identified the commands needed for initialization and service selection.

entries.stdout: Docs confirm `stripe projects init [name]`, `.projects/` state files, service catalog browsing, and supported providers including Vercel, Railway, Supabase, Neon, PlanetScale, Turso, Chroma, Clerk, PostHog, and Runloop.
entries.reasoning_summary: Used the official Stripe docs to ground the workflow and avoid guessing about supported providers or CLI behavior.

entries.stdout: Catalog returned live provider/service data. Relevant free or low-friction categories present include compute (Vercel, Railway), database (Neon, Railway, Supabase, Turso), vector/AI database (Chroma), analytics (PostHog), and auth (Clerk, Supabase, Neon).
entries.reasoning_summary: Fetched live catalog data so provider selection can be randomized from the current available set rather than hard-coded.

entries.stdout: Error JSON_REQUIRES_AUTH: No authenticated Stripe session found. Re-run without `--json` to complete browser authentication first.
entries.reasoning_summary: Tested whether the project could be initialized non-interactively. The result shows Stripe authentication is currently the blocking step for live provisioning.

decisions: Use the exact naming scheme `{AGENT}_{MODEL}-transcript_objects-{enum}` under `session_logs/` for audit artifacts.
Record concise decision summaries instead of private raw chain-of-thought.
Treat remote provider provisioning as optional at implementation time if authentication remains unavailable; keep the app runnable locally.
